question: How is a Camunda Form associated with a user task in Camunda 8?

options.text: It must always be embedded inline in the BPMN XML.
By a form key / form ID on the user task; the form may be embedded in the deployment or linked by ID.
By writing HTML in a documentation annotation on the task.
By a runtime API call from Tasklist after the user opens the task.

a.text: Inline embedding is not required. Forms are referenced from the user task by key or id and can be deployed independently.

b.text: User tasks reference a form by key or id. The form itself can be deployed alongside the BPMN (embedded form) or referenced by id (linked form). Tasklist renders the referenced form at runtime.

c.text: Documentation annotations are descriptive metadata and do not render a form.

d.text: Tasklist does not associate forms at runtime; the link is part of the deployed user task definition.

explanation: A user task references a Camunda Form by a form key or form id. The form can be embedded in the same deployment or linked by id; Tasklist renders the referenced form when the user opens the task.
